response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack engineer with broad web, backend, and some embedded experience, but is a poor match for this role. The position has hard requirements that are absent: native-level Japanese proficiency (the resume lists only English, Romanian, and basic German), strong C for UNIX development (the candidate's C/C++ work is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments years ago, with primary expertise in TypeScript/Node/DLang), and Linux kernel investigation/source modification. There is also no evidence of network-security or LAN-switch domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, NETCONF/SNMP).
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering background across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded systems
Strong testing/TDD discipline and DevOps/CI-CD experience
Some prior C/C++ experience building a C IDE toolchain at National Instruments
concerns: No Japanese language ability, which is a stated native-level requirement
Minimal and dated C-for-UNIX experience; core stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang
No demonstrated Linux kernel work or embedded networking security domain knowledge
